info.title: Torque — Vehicle Service API (vehapiproxi)
info.description:
Comprehensive API for the **Torque** app: Motor M1 proxy (`sites.motor.com/m1`), Database-backed caches, Stripe credits, AI (Nemotron), and L2 search.

**Torque auth (credits, L2, article metadata, Motor Information YMME):** `Authorization: Bearer <Firebase access_token>`. The proxy rejects `x-user-id`; use a real JWT.

**Motor upstream auth:** Most `/api/...` passthrough routes require the proxy’s EBSCO session (managed server-side); browser clients call the **Cloud Run proxy** origin only.

**Architecture Notes:**
* **Environments:** This specification covers both the upstream Motor.com service and the custom Cloud Run Proxy. 
* **Routing/Sharding:** The `{contentSource}` parameter acts as a primary shard key, routing requests to specific OEM (Toyota, Ford) or aftermarket (MOTOR) databases.
* **ID Hierarchy:** `{vehicleId}` represents the Base Model. `motorVehicleId` (passed as a query parameter) represents the highly specific Engine/Trim level.

**Consumption guide (practical call flow):**
* **Step 1 — Resolve Year/Make/Model:** `GET /api/years` → `GET /api/year/{year}/makes` → `GET /api/year/{year}/make/{make}/models`
  - The **models response carries `contentSource`** (this is authoritative; do not guess from make name).
* **Step 2 — Pick the vehicleId:** Choose a model row from `models` and use its `vehicleId` (format varies by source; treat as opaque string).
* **Step 3 — If engine/trim is required:** For many non-`MOTOR` sources, call
  `GET /api/source/{contentSource}/{vehicleId}/motorvehicles` and select an engine; pass the selected engine id as `motorVehicleId` on downstream calls.
* **Step 4 — Content:** Use the `/api/source/{contentSource}/vehicle/{vehicleId}/...` endpoints.
* **Toyota Delta mode:** When comparing quarters, the client uses `contentSource=ToyotaDelta` and calls `toyotadeltaarticles`
  with `sourceQuarter` + `targetQuarter` (instead of `articles/v2`).

**M1 connector frontend (reference):** Parameter usage and response shapes for `sites.motor.com/m1` are documented in **`vehapiproxi/API_CONSUMPTION_DOCUMENTATION.md`** (e.g. `searchTerm` on search/parts/maintenance, `frequencyTypeCode` F/N/R for maintenance-by-frequency, labor only for `L:` article IDs, placeholders `-997`/`-998`/`-999`).

**Repo:** Also published as `documentation/vehapiproxi-openapi.yaml` (generated from this file).

servers.description: Upstream MOTOR Server (direct — not used by Torque SPA)

tags.name: Proxy
tags.description: Proxy-specific endpoints for health checks, authentication management, and debug logging

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Operations for session management and authentication

tags.name: Credits
tags.description: Operations for managing user credits, purchasing via Stripe, and unlocking features

tags.name: Vehicles
tags.description: Operations for vehicle information and selection (Years, Makes, Models, Trims)

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Operations for searching articles and content across buckets

tags.name: Assets
tags.description: Operations for retrieving specific articles, graphics, and typed asset collections (TSBs, DTCs, etc.)

tags.name: Parts
tags.description: Operations for retrieving parts information and catalogs

tags.name: Maintenance Schedules
tags.description: Maintenance schedules (indicators, frequency, intervals). M1 issues **separate** frequency requests per `frequencyTypeCode` (`F`, `N`, `R`) and passes `searchTerm` + `motorVehicleId` like other vehicle routes. See `vehapiproxi/API_CONSUMPTION_DOCUMENTATION.md`.

tags.name: Track Change
tags.description: Operations for tracking changes and delta reports

tags.name: UI
tags.description: Operations for UI configuration, assets, and settings

tags.name: AI
tags.description: Nemotron-backed rewrite, tutorials, and common-issues generation

tags.name: L2 RAG
tags.description: Vector search over normalized content chunks (requires unlock + embeddings)

tags.name: Motor Information
tags.description: Optional `api.motor.com` Information API — YMME base vehicle, engines, recommended fluids

tags.name: Article access
tags.description: Article metadata for paywall / module resolution

get.description: Returns proxy health status including whether the upstream Motor session is valid

lastAuth.description: Epoch ms of last successful auth

llmKeyConfigured.description: True when NVIDIA_API_KEY / LLM_API_KEY is set (value never returned)

llmKeyEnv.description: Which env var name provides the LLM key (for deployment verification)

get.description: Returns buffered proxy request/response logs with optional filtering
Returns the current authentication status and progress.

post.summary: Verify Stripe checkout session after redirect

post.description: Raw JSON body with `Stripe-Signature`. Called by Stripe only — not for browsers.

503.description: AI module unavailable or missing LLM API key

post.summary: Generate tutorial steps from article HTML
Generate common issues for a vehicle

vehicleId.description: External vehicle id for Supabase context enrichment

403.description: No module unlock for this vehicle

post.summary: L2 semantic search (legacy — vehicle in body)

get.summary: Resolve Motor Information baseVehicleId from YMME
List engines for YMME (Motor Information API)

post.description: Log client side errors without injecting the external logging url.

200.description: Java-style properties file for i18n support.

get.description: All vehicles matching the provided year and make. Returns `contentSource`.

Motor vehicle details (engine/trim variants) for a selected `vehicleId`.

**When to call this:**
- If `contentSource != MOTOR`, the M1 frontend typically calls this endpoint after a model is selected
  so the user can choose an engine/trim. The chosen engine id is then passed as `motorVehicleId` to other endpoints.
- If `contentSource == MOTOR`, clients usually skip this step (the `vehicleId` is already engine-specific).

get.summary: Get search results by vehicle ID
get.description:
Metadata for articles that match the provided vehicle and search term. Returns geo-blocking/entitlement data.

**Parameters:**
- `searchTerm` is optional (empty means "all buckets").
- `motorVehicleId` is optional, but may be **required** for some `contentSource` values where `vehicleId` is model-level
  and engine selection is separate (use `/motorvehicles` to discover valid values).

**Response (M1):** JSON envelope with `header` and `body` where `body` contains `articleDetails`, `filterTabs`, and optional `vehicleGeoBlockingDetails` (see `vehapiproxi/API_CONSUMPTION_DOCUMENTATION.md`).

**Toyota Delta mode:** do not use this endpoint when comparing quarters — use `toyotadeltaarticles`.

get.summary: Get Toyota Delta search results by vehicle ID
get.description:
Metadata for Toyota Delta articles that match the provided vehicle and search term.

The M1 frontend uses this endpoint when `sourceQuarter` and `targetQuarter` are both provided, and forces
`contentSource=ToyotaDelta`.
Fetches available vehicle configurations/orientations for articles that require selection (e.g. engine variants)
Content and relevant metadata for a specific article.
The raw XML content of an article. Included for debugging purposes.

M1 passes optional `searchTerm` (often empty) for server-side parts filtering. Include `motorVehicleId` when required for the selected `contentSource`.

See `vehapiproxi/API_CONSUMPTION_DOCUMENTATION.md`.

Returns labor/estimated work time data for an article.
**M1 requirement:** `searchTerm` must be present (use empty string if no search).
**articleId:** Use **`L:`**-prefixed labor IDs only. P: (Procedure) and L: (Labor) are different ID spaces;
procedure P:551733272 has labor at L:25760276, not L:551733272.
**Placeholders:** virtual article IDs `-997`, `-998`, `-999` are **not** labor-detail targets (handled elsewhere in the M1 UI).

See `vehapiproxi/API_CONSUMPTION_DOCUMENTATION.md`.

The M1 connector calls this **once per** `frequencyTypeCode` (`F`, `N`, `R` — fixed / normal / related severities) to populate maintenance-by-frequency UI (`MaintenanceSchedulesFacade.searchByFrequency`). Pass `motorVehicleId` when `vehicleId` is model-level (e.g. `GeneralMotors`).

See `vehapiproxi/API_CONSUMPTION_DOCUMENTATION.md`.

parameters.name: frequencyTypeCode
parameters.description: Single-letter frequency bucket. M1 frontend issues separate requests for `F`, `N`, and `R`.

get.description:
Supports `intervalType` + `interval` (e.g. Miles + 5000). M1 passes `searchTerm` (often empty) and `motorVehicleId` when required. Works across `contentSource` values that expose maintenance data—not limited to `MOTOR`.

See `vehapiproxi/API_CONSUMPTION_DOCUMENTATION.md`.

M1 `MaintenanceSchedulesFacade.searchByIndicators`. Pass `motorVehicleId` when the path `vehicleId` is model-level.

See `vehapiproxi/API_CONSUMPTION_DOCUMENTATION.md`.
Resolves saved bookmark content. Response may include `isOutdated` when upstream indicates the article changed since the bookmark was created (`vehapiproxi/API_CONSUMPTION_DOCUMENTATION.md`).

post.description: Log comparisons between different revisions of technical documents.

RateLimitLimit.description: Maximum number of requests permitted per interval.

RateLimitRemaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current interval.

RateLimitReset.description: Unix timestamp indicating when the rate limit window resets.

VehicleIdParam.name: vehicleId
VehicleIdParam.description:
Vehicle ID selected from the models list.

Treat this as an **opaque identifier**. Its internal format varies by `contentSource`:
- `MOTOR`: often looks like `modelId:engineId`
- Other sources: often model-level, with engine selection represented separately by `motorVehicleId`

MotorVehicleIdQuery.name: motorVehicleId
MotorVehicleIdQuery.description:
Engine/trim identifier for precise sharding (example: `79612:3016`).

**How to obtain:**
- Call `GET /api/source/{contentSource}/{vehicleId}/motorvehicles` and select an engine `id`.

**When to use:**
- Often required for non-`MOTOR` content sources.
- Typically omitted for `MOTOR` where `vehicleId` already encodes engine/trim.

SearchTermQuery.name: searchTerm
SearchTermQuery.description:
Server-side filter on catalog/search routes. **M1 UI** passes this query param on articles/v2, parts, maintenance schedules, and labor (often as an empty string for “all”). Optional; omit vs `''` behavior may match upstream defaults.

See `vehapiproxi/API_CONSUMPTION_DOCUMENTATION.md`.

SourceQuarterQuery.name: sourceQuarter
SourceQuarterQuery.description:
Source quarter for Toyota Delta mode (e.g. `2024Q4`).

When both `sourceQuarter` and `targetQuarter` are provided, clients should call `toyotadeltaarticles`
with `contentSource=ToyotaDelta`.

TargetQuarterQuery.name: targetQuarter
TargetQuarterQuery.description:
Target quarter for Toyota Delta mode (e.g. `2025Q1`).

When both `sourceQuarter` and `targetQuarter` are provided, clients should call `toyotadeltaarticles`
with `contentSource=ToyotaDelta`.

ContentSource.description:
Vehicle data provider enumeration.

Authoritative list comes from the M1 frontend `ContentSource` enum.

BucketName.description: Categories for document buckets mapping directly to the backend tags.

DocumentState.description: Represents the version of the document.

ArticleResponse.description: Article detail payload. **Upstream/M1** commonly returns `html`, optional base64 `pdf`, `documentId`, dates, and silo arrays (`vehapiproxi/API_CONSUMPTION_DOCUMENTATION.md`). The **proxy/Torque** may normalize into `content` / `title` for app consumption.

html.description: Raw or processed HTML from Motor when applicable

pdf.description: Base64-encoded PDF when the article is PDF-backed

isOutdated.description: Present for bookmarks / revision tracking when upstream supplies it

MakeListItem.description: Make row as returned by M1 `GET /api/year/{year}/makes`.

MakeListResponse.description: List of makes. M1 typically returns `items` as `{ makeId, makeName }` objects; string-only entries may appear in some environments.

ModelRow.description: Single model line from `GET /api/year/{year}/make/{make}/models`. Use `id` (M1) or legacy `vehicleId` as the path vehicle identifier; for `MOTOR`, prefer `engines[].id` when engines are present.

id.description: Primary vehicle identifier from M1 models API

vehicleId.description: Alternate field name in some clients

ModelsResponseResponse.description: Models for a year/make. **`contentSource` is authoritative** for all subsequent `/api/source/{contentSource}/vehicle/...` calls.

ModelAndVehicleIdListResponse.description: Model and vehicle ID list response (also functions as VehicleSelectionResponseBody)

VehicleGeoBlockingDetails.description: Geo / entitlement flags on articles/v2 (M1 may show a blocking modal).

SearchResultsBody.description: `body` object inside Motor/M1 JSON for `articles/v2` (and Toyota Delta articles). Matches M1 `SearchResultsResponse` usage in `vehapiproxi/API_CONSUMPTION_DOCUMENTATION.md`.

articleDetails.description: Article rows used by the M1 search store (same shape as `Article` in this spec).

SearchResultsResponse.description:
Motor/M1 envelope for `GET .../articles/v2` and `.../toyotadeltaarticles`. The M1 connector reads **`body.articleDetails`** and **`body.filterTabs`**. Some proxies or clients may forward an **unwrapped** `body` object only.

**Note:** Older/incorrect specs sometimes showed a top-level `results` array—that is **not** the upstream M1 shape.

partDescription.description: Human-readable description of the part (common key from Motor/M1 responses).

description.description: Same semantic as `partDescription` when the API returns this property name instead.

isAdded.description: When true, part is associated with a labor operation in the M1 UI

ArticleContent.description: Article content containing HTML. The proxy normalizes this content.

BearerAuth.description: Supabase Auth access_token (used for credits, L2, article metadata, Motor Information YMME).
